lever_summary: Dense multi-dim broadcast via an incremental BroadcastOdometer. broadcast_binary_tensors decoded each output element's multi-index (flat_to_multi_into + two broadcast_flat_index dot-products) and gathered Literals from materialized Vec<Literal>. The odometer maintains both broadcast source offsets incrementally; broadcast_binary_f64 gains a dense as_f64_slice() tier and a new broadcast_binary_i64 folds as_i64_slice() (no i64 broadcast fast path existed before — i64 went fully generic).

alien_graveyard.primitive: strength reduction of per-element address arithmetic (incremental index odometer) + dense SoA gather
alien_graveyard.mapping: the broadcast analog of the reduction OutIndexOdometer (mcqr.30.11/.12): a carry odometer advances both operand offsets in O(1) amortized while gathering from contiguous typed slices

method: Same-worker comparison on ts2: dense (new_*_values / vector_*) inputs vs Vec<Literal>-backed reference benches run in the same process. The f64 literal reference exercises the prior materialize+decode f64 broadcast path; the i64 literal reference exercises the fully generic binary_literal_op broadcast loop.

add_broadcast_256_i64.note: headline: i64 broadcast previously had no fast path (per-element binary_literal_op with promote/try_from)

add_broadcast_256_f64.note: improves the existing f64 broadcast path by removing the per-element decode + Literal materialization

command: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- add_broadcast_256 --save-baseline bcast

change.lever: Added struct BroadcastOdometer { dims, lhs_strides, rhs_strides, coord, lhs_idx, rhs_idx } with next() -> (usize, usize). broadcast_binary_f64 gains a leading dense as_f64_slice() + odometer tier (Literal-gather path retained as fallback). New broadcast_binary_i64 (dense as_i64_slice() + odometer) wired into broadcast_binary_tensors after the f64 attempt; both return None (generic fallback) when not dense.

isomorphism.ordering: unchanged: output produced in the same ascending row-major flat order; the odometer reproduces broadcast_flat_index(flat_to_multi(flat), strides) for both operands (broadcast-aware strides are 0 on broadcast axes)
isomorphism.op: unchanged: same float_op / int_op carrying per-primitive semantics applied to the gathered values; for I64xI64 binary_literal_op returns Literal::I64(int_op(a,b)) and promote_dtype(I64,I64)==I64, for F64xF64 Literal::from_f64(float_op(a,b))
isomorphism.errors: dense tiers fire only when both operands expose as_f64_slice()/as_i64_slice() (well-formed dense storage); Literal-backed / mixed-dtype / promoting cases fall back to the unchanged generic broadcast loop
isomorphism.output_dtype: F64 (dense f64 path) / I64 (dense i64 path), matching the generic promote_dtype result

validation.name: dense_broadcast_parity
validation.result: passed: 1052 tests incl new dense_broadcast_bit_identical_to_literal_path (5 broadcast shapes x Add/Sub/Mul, f64 + i64)

validation.name: clippy_deny_warnings
validation.command: rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fj-lax --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings
